description: Functional divisions of the Blue Hen RE synthetic organization — ownership, handoffs, and closed loop. Normative companion to specs/0012.

divisions.name: Data Harvest & Curation Org
divisions.mission: Mine, ingest, clean, chunk, and pair tenant corpora so Research never trains on garbage.
owns: Corpus freshness SLAs and data-gap backlog

orchestration: Prioritized harvest requests with site_id, source URLs, and acceptance criteria
Feedback on corpus gaps from production eval slices
research: Pair schema requirements and minimum doc counts for experiments
Labeling guidelines when supervised pairs are needed
execution: Production query logs (future) for hard-negative mining — optional Phase A+

research: Data quality report: doc count, dedupe rate, avg chunk length, pair count
orchestration: Acknowledgment of harvest completion or blocked reason

sla.phaseA: ≥8 docs, ≥64 pairs per Phase A tenant within 24h of harvest request
sla.quality: No empty chunks; UTF-8 clean; workspace RLS enforced

divisions.name: Embedding Research Org
divisions.mission: Invent, ablate, and measure embedding methods; promote honest candidates to BD.
divisions.owner: Research Lead (agents: Cursor lab, Claude autoresearch, Conductor)
doesNotOwn: Raw web scraping (delegates to Data Org)

data: Paired, chunked corpus ready for train_asn
Minimum pair count met per SLA
bd: Commercial baseline panel results for calibration
orchestration: Weakest-slice signal from Conductor / Eve hill_climb

bd: Recipe candidate package: recipe JSON, checkpoint path, eval-harness gates, EVIDENCE snapshot date
Promotion criteria checklist (real-text ΔnDCG, OOD forgetting, edge tier knn_t8)
orchestration: Train job enqueue with validated recipe
data: Data requests when eval shows slice starvation (e.g. need more arXiv CS.CL papers)

sla.promotion: Only enqueue BD queue when EVIDENCE gate documented
sla.honesty: No ship claims without JSON snapshot date in EVIDENCE.md

divisions.name: Business Development & Validation Org
divisions.mission: Stress-test Research candidates on real-world scenarios vs commercial baselines; charter winners for Execution.
owns: Real-world scenario catalog (multi-hop, domain shift, edge latency)

research: BD queue candidates with full eval artifact bundle
Honest limitations doc (in-domain vs zero-shot caveats)
execution: Deployed model version id for A/B in pilot
Latency and error rates during pilot window
data: Holdout eval corpora not used in training (disjoint slice)

execution: Scorecard JSON with pass/fail per exam
research: Failure analysis: which exam, which baseline won, suggested data or recipe fix
orchestration: Escalation if charter blocked on cost ceiling

sla.pilot: Complete scorecard within 5 business days of queue entry
sla.fairness: Same exam for org model and commercial baselines; disclose in-domain bias

divisions.name: Business Implementation & Execution Org
divisions.mission: Deploy approved models, serve embeddings at scale, and feed production truth back to the loop.
divisions.owner: Platform SRE (services/worker, core-api, Modal trainer TBD)

bd: Approved execution charter with recipe JSON
research: Checkpoint compatible with asn-engine train_loop output
data: Indexed chunks for search after deploy

orchestration: Production metric deltas (nDCG on rotating slice, p95 latency)
data: Query logs and retrieval misses (future) for hard-negative mining

sla.deploy: Charter → deployed + indexed within 4h on Phase A stack
sla.serving: RFC 9457 errors; RLS isolation verified in CI

divisions.name: Orchestration & Intelligence Org
divisions.mission: Close the loop — detect gaps, route work, enforce budgets, and keep divisions aligned.
divisions.owner: Operator + Eve Chief of Staff (apps/synthorg, apps/control)
owns: Data-gap backlog routing to Data Org

data: Harvest completion or SLA breach notice
execution: Fleet health and slice weakness signals

all: Prioritized work orders via ledger + Eve tools
Weekly weakest-slice report on control plane

sla.response: Gap detected → data request logged within 1 conductor cycle

steps: Platform Orchestration identifies a gap, new vertical, or weak production signal
Data Operations ingests, cleans, chunks, and generates training pairs
Research & Development trains, evaluates, and records evidence
R&D submits a candidate to the Validation Queue
Validation & Charter runs certified benchmarks vs commercial baselines
Validation issues a production charter on pass
Production deploys the model, builds the vector index, and serves retrieval
Production and Validation publish metrics back to Orchestration
Orchestration opens data requests → back to Data Operations
